content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 26, 1988


Evet Abt
Chief Deputy City Attorney
151 West Mission Steet
San Jose, CA 95110

Re: 88-407

Dear Mr. Abt:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on October 21, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                    Date : October 28, 1988


From : Fair Political Practices Commission
       John McLean

Subject: T-88-407

       Spoke today with Evet Abt, Chief Deputy City Attorney in San
       Jose.  Informed her that her letter was an accurate reflection
       of the advice I had provided.  I.e. The names of elected
       officers could appear in a roster listing in bond documents.
       The bond documents contain no other reference to elected
       officials.  She said that she did not need a written response.

JM:ld:T88407

CITY OF SAN JOSÉ, CALIFORNIA
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

151 WEST MISSION STREET
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95110
(408) 277-4454

JOAN R. GALLO                                    October 18, 1988
City Attorney


          JOHN McLEAN
          COUNSEL, LEGAL DIVISION
          FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
          428 J Street, Suite 800
          P.O. Box 807
          Sacramento, CA    95804

               RE:  Roster Listings

          Dear Mr. McLean:

               This letter is to confirm that you advised our
          office by telephone on 14 October 1988 that the roster
          listing as described below may be included in documents
          sent at public expense without violating Government
          Code Section 89001.

               As we have explained, a single page length roster
          listing containing the names of City employees and
          elected officials will be included in the official
          statements accompanying municipal bond documents.  It is
          expected that beginning later this year, more than 200
          such documents in a calendar month will be distributed
          at public expense to interested investors. The names
          of elected officers are grouped separately, as are the
          names of Council appointees and City employees involved
          in the project to be funded.  However, the names of
          elected officers are in the same type size and typeface
          as others listed.  The bond documents contain no other
          reference to elected officials.

               It is our understanding that you advise that Govern-
          ment Code Section 89001 permits the inclusion of such a
          roster listing in the bond documents.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        EVET ABT
                                        Chief Deputy City Attorney

          EA:MAM;mjw

sections.question: Whether a roster listing containing the names of elected officials can be included in bond documents distributed at public expense without violating Government Code Section 89001.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a city include a roster listing of elected officials in official statements accompanying municipal bond documents distributed at public expense without violating Government Code Section 89001?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter (dated October 18, 1988) from City Attorney, with an FPPC acknowledgment letter (October 26, 1988) and follow-up memo (October 28, 1988) indicating informal phone advice was provided. The informal advice confirmed roster listings were permissible, but no formal written response was requested. The file contains the original request and agency acknowledgment but not a formal FPPC advice letter.

embedding.summary: San Jose Assistant City Attorney Evet Abt requests FPPC advice on whether a roster listing containing names of elected officials can be included in bond documents distributed at public expense. The city subsequently confirms via phone advice that such roster listings are permitted under Government Code Section 89001.
